name: Roasted Brussels Sprouts Pavé with Aged Balsamic Emulsion and Crispy Leaves
description: A structural culinary masterpiece featuring precisely layered and compressed Brussels sprouts, slow-roasted to achieve velvet-tender hearts with glass-like crispy exteriors. Finished with a glossy aged balsamic reduction, lemon zest, and accompanied by a light truffle-scented foam and crispy sprout leaf chips.
keywords: brussels sprouts, michelin style, roasted pavé, aged balsamic, molecular gastronomy, haute cuisine

ingredients.note: thinly sliced on a Japanese mandoline

ingredients.item: toasted Marcona almonds and pine nuts

steps.instruction: Trim Brussels sprouts and separate outer leaves for crispy chips. Halve the remaining sprouts lengthwise. Blanch halves in heavily salted boiling water for 2-3 minutes, shock in ice bath, and dry thoroughly using a high-capacity salad spinner.
Using a Japanese mandoline, slice shallots paper-thin. Toss sprout halves and reserved leaves separately with clarified butter, avocado oil, crushed garlic, thyme, sea salt, and pepper.

Preheat a heavy carbon steel or All-Clad baking sheet in a 230°C (450°F) oven. Arrange sprout halves cut-side down in a single layer. Vacuum-seal the layered halves in a chamber vacuum sealer for compression if desired, or arrange tightly in a terrine mold.

Roast at 230°C for 20-25 minutes until deeply caramelized with mahogany edges. Remove crispy leaves earlier when golden and set aside. In the final 5 minutes, brush lightly with aged balsamic for extra glaze.

Prepare the emulsion: Reduce aged balsamic with lemon juice and a touch of honey. For an aerated touch, warm heavy cream with truffle oil, blend, and charge in a Gourmet Whip with one N2O canister. Hold at 60°C.
Portioning: Cut the roasted pavé into precise rectangular portions or serve whole halves. Sear cut sides again briefly in a hot carbon steel pan for extra crispness.

Plating: Place the Brussels pavé centerpiece on a pristine white plate. Dispense a delicate cloud of truffle emulsion beside it. Artfully arrange crispy sprout leaves, toasted nuts, grated Parmigiano, lemon zest, and Maldon flakes. Finish with micro-thyme and a final balsamic drizzle.

specialtools.item: Heavy Carbon Steel or All-Clad Baking Sheet

winePairings.name: Vintage Champagne
winePairings.notes: High acidity cuts through the rich caramelization and highlights the earthy truffle notes.

winePairings.name: Meursault or Puligny-Montrachet
winePairings.notes: Buttery Chardonnay mirrors the roasted depth and balances the balsamic brightness.
